channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Argentina's Polarizing Football Identity and Global Rivalries in the FIFA World Cup
item.description: The article explores Argentina's complex football identity shaped by legends like Diego Maradona and Lionel Messi, which has made the nation a polarizing force in international football. Argentina's h...

item.title: Spain and Argentina Clash in 2026 FIFA World Cup Final
item.description: Spain and Argentina are set to face off in the 2026 FIFA World Cup final on Sunday, with both teams aiming for historic victories. Spain seeks their second men's title after winning Euro 2024, while A...

item.title: Rivers Senator Urges Youth to Embrace Skills for National Development
item.description: Senator Ipalibo Harry Banigo, representing Rivers West, addressed 140 youths who recently completed training in lithium-ion-based street light installation and maintenance. The senator emphasized that...

item.title: B.C. Wildfires Surge Past 100 as Lightning Sparks Intense Fire Season
item.description: British Columbia is experiencing an unprecedented wildfire crisis, with the number of active fires surpassing 100 due to a surge in lightning strikes. The BC Wildfire Service reports that over 4,000 l...

item.title: Pakistan's Finance Minister Engages US on Trade Negotiations and Tariff Reductions
item.description: Finance Minister Muhammad Aurangzeb has arrived in Washington for a three-day visit aimed at advancing bilateral economic ties with the United States. During his trip, he will engage with U.S. officia...

item.title: Skyroot's Vikram-1 successfully completes India's first private orbital launch, marking a major milestone for the country's commercial space sector
item.description: India achieved a significant milestone in its space programme after Skyroot Aerospace successfully completed the maiden orbital flight of its privately developed Vikram-1 rocket from the Satish Dhawan...

item.title: Cape Town tackles fake license plates linked to organized crime
item.description: Cape Town authorities are intensifying efforts to combat luxury vehicles with fraudulent registration plates linked to organized crime. Recent arrests, including Nigerian nationals driving a Mercedes-...

item.title: Winners and losers of the 2026 World Cup: A balanced assessment of triumphs and challenges
item.description: The article examines the multifaceted outcomes of the 2026 FIFA World Cup, highlighting both successes and challenges across sports, politics, and infrastructure. Host nations like Canada, the U.S., a...

item.title: SpaceX Open-Sources Grok Build Tool After Privacy Concerns and Data Retention Revisions
item.description: SpaceX has open-sourced its Grok Build CLI tool following significant privacy concerns and data retention adjustments. Initially, the AI tool faced criticism for uploading users' entire repositories t...

item.title: Communication expert urges ethical digital engagement to strengthen electoral integrity ahead of future elections
item.description: Communication expert John Best Uche has stressed that responsible communication is essential to protecting electoral integrity, especially as digital platforms continue to shape public opinion and dem...

item.title: Kenyan Man's Battle with Rare Cancer and Advocacy for Research
item.description: Roy Mugera, a 32-year-old barman from Dagenham, died after a two-year fight with synovial sarcoma, a rare cancer affecting fewer than 80 people in the UK annually. His ordeal began in 2023 when persis...

item.title: Indonesian Boat Sinking: Five Survivors Found Amid Search for 20 Missing
item.description: A passenger boat sank in eastern Indonesia waters, resulting in multiple casualties and ongoing search efforts. The KM Nurul Salsa, which was carrying 78 passengers and crew, experienced engine failur...

item.title: Madlanga Commission examines conflicting police evidence, missing cocaine and Carrim's postponed testimony
item.description: The Madlanga Commission of Inquiry spent the week examining conflicting evidence from police officers, the disappearance of 27kg of cocaine from a Hawks evidence store, and the postponement of testimo...

item.title: Managing Family Stress from an Uncle’s Cognitive Decline
item.description: The article discusses the challenges faced by Amy, who is dealing with her uncle's cognitive decline and the stress of having him live with her family. She seeks advice on balancing caregiving respons...

item.title: CBSE Class 10 second board exams see improved pass rates and student performance
item.description: The Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) announced that the overall pass percentage for Class 10 students in the second board examination rose to 96.78%, up from 93.66% in 2025. A total of 59.9...

item.title: Databricks Secures $188 Billion Valuation as AI Transition Accelerates
item.description: Databricks, a data analytics company, announced a $188 billion valuation following a new funding round led by Coatue. The company, which transitioned from a big data SaaS provider to an AI-focused ent...

item.title: Mel C Weds Australian Model in Victoria Beckham-Designed Wedding Gown
item.description: Melanie Chisholm, known as Sporty Spice from the Spice Girls, has married Australian model Chris Dingwall in a wedding designed by Victoria Beckham. The ceremony took place at Country House Cumbria in...

item.title: Youths Demand Handouts, Police Use Tear Gas in Kitale
item.description: The article details a tense incident in Kitale where youths demanded immediate financial support from government officials after a boda boda empowerment event. The event, organized under the governmen...

item.title: Tornado Alerts Lifted in Southern Ontario as Thunderstorm Watches Remain in Effect
item.description: Tornado alerts have been lifted in Southern Ontario, but thunderstorm watches remain in effect as weather conditions continue to shift. The Canadian government has issued updated advisories, with live...

item.title: Alcohol tax reforms aim to curb excessive consumption and illicit trade
item.description: The South African alcohol industry has urged the National Treasury to link new excise levies to consumer price inflation and incentivize production of lower-strength alcoholic drinks to address abuse ...
